source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The edges of rectangle $P Q R S$ are parallel to the coordinate axes. $P Q R S$ lies below the $x$-axis and to the right of the $y$-axis as shown in the diagram. The coordinates of $P, Q, R$ and $S$ are all integers. For each point, we calculate the value $(y$-coordinate $) \div(x$-coordinate $)$. Which of the four points gives the least value? <image1>

multi_step: solving requires comparing the quantity (y-coordinate)/(x-coordinate) across four rectangle vertices rather than reading off a single fact
the reasoning combines geometric/visual information (rectangle position relative to axes and vertex labels) with numeric comparison of signed ratios
the image must be interpreted first, then the candidate points must be compared to determine the least value
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text is a single discrete label ('P')
registry_passed=true, with question, answer, and image all present
the expected output is a finite multiple-choice selection, which is straightforward to check once the diagram interpretation is fixed
